commands.author: sercada

target.title: feat(doctor): warn about hidden provider catalog models
target.body:
## Summary
- mark provider-supplied supplemental model catalog rows with stable catalog provenance
- add a warn-only doctor contribution for explicit agents.defaults.models allowlists hiding provider-supplied catalog models
- classify the new doctor contribution in the current doctor health conversion plan
- keep opt-in guidance warn-only; no allowlist is changed automatically

Fixes #39992.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor` did not warn when an explicit `agents.defaults.models` allowlist hid provider-supplied supplemental catalog rows, so newly exposed provider models could be unavailable without operator guidance.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `bb8c98989e`, using the repo test runner against the real doctor contribution and model catalog modules.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout:

```text
$ pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 30.10s

unit-fast: 1 file passed, 22 tests passed
unit: 1 file passed, 3 tests passed
agents: 1 file passed, 29 tests passed

$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: The real doctor contribution list now includes `doctor:model-allowlist-catalog`, the conversion-plan invariant passes, and the model-catalog/doctor warning coverage confirms hidden provider-supplemental rows produce opt-in guidance without mutating config.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps in the targeted doctor warning path; full repository CI covers build and regression breadth.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this follow-up, CI failed `checks-node-core-src-security` in `src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts` because `doctor:model-allowlist-catalog` was present in contributions but missing from `doctorHealthConversionRules`.

## Testing
- pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts
- git diff --check
target.author: sercada
